definitions.text: To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.
To send out as in a stream or a flood; to cause (an emotion) to come out; to cause to escape.
examples: My teacher poured scorn on my attempts at writing.

definitions.text: To send forth from, as in a stream; to discharge uninterruptedly.

examples: The bartender's inexpert pour left me with a pint of beer that was half foam.

definitions.text: A downpour; a flood of precipitation.
